title: Sam Kerr & Matildas Prepare For Iran Match | 10 Sport
description: Sam Kerr has expressed sympathy for the Matildas' next Women's Asian Cup opponents Iran, following recent developments in their home country.  "Obviously it's really tough what's going on around the world. But you know, they're just young, young girls and young footballers ... we'll treat the game like any other game and show the team the utmost respect and prepare properly," Kerr said following Australia's win over the Philippines to open the tournament. title: US-Iran Conflict Leaves 115,000 Australians Stranded As Travel Chaos Continues | 10 News
description: The Middle East's latest war is widening at a rapid pace tonight with more countries being drawn into the conflict, which President Donald Trump has declared could last weeks. The death toll has surged in Iran, with reports that more than 200 people have been killed in the joint US-Israeli attacks. We are learning more tonight about just how many Australians have been caught up in the conflict. The backlog from travel chaos across the world will take days to clear. title: Iran Hits Oil Refinery, Fuel Price Fears, Thousands Stranded – 10’s Late News
description: Tonight on 10’s Late News – More than 500 people have been killed in Iran following joint US-Israeli strikes, thousands of Australians have been stranded at airports, an Iranian drone has hit a major oil refinery in Saudi Arabia, there are fears the Middle East conflict could send fuel prices soaring, a man has been rescued from a croc-infested river in north Queensland, Delta Goodrem will represent Australia at Eurovision, a shark has been spotted more than 20 kilometres from the beach in Sydney, the Matildas have landed in Queensland ahead of Thursday’s match with Iran, another suspected shadow fleet tanker has been seized in the North Sea, a hit and run has been captured on camera in Brazil, Pauline Hanson has been censured by the Senate over her controversial comments about Muslims, a prehistoric human skeleton has been discovered in an underwater cave along Mexico’s Caribbean coast, a Michelangelo masterpiece is getting a ‘facial’ in the Sistine Chapel, and a monkey has been found hanging out inside a garage in the US.
